---
title: "411A Advisor Emails"
format: 
  html:
    embed-resources: true
---

## SUBJECT

MEDS Capstone Updates - Winter Week X

## Week 2

Hello MEDS Capstone Advisors, 

I hope you are doing great.

This is your weekly EDS 411 - MEDS Capstone project newsletter. Please feel free to reach out with any questions or suggestions.

Best wishes, 

Carmen 
 
{{< include /_email_signature.qmd >}}

---

[EDS 411 Course website]({{< var 411-course-website >}}){style="color: #0066cc;"}

**[Capstone guidelines]({{< var capstone-guidelines >}}){style="color: #0066cc;"}**

<span style="font-weight: bold;">Upcoming Major Deadlines </span>

![](/411a_winter/images/411a_deadlines1.png)


::: {style="border: 2px solid #ff6600; padding: 15px; background-color: #fff5e6; border-radius: 5px;"}
<span style="font-weight: bold;">Advisor Check-ins</span>

- All groups should get their advisor's feedback and approval on their project summary before submitting it this {{< var website-project-abstract-deadline >}}.

- Students might reach out about feedback on section VII of the [Team Management Plan]({{< var team-management-plan-template >}}){style="color: #0066cc;"}. This section outlines overall work expectations between team members and their faculty advisor.

- Students will start working on their Design and Implementation Plan this week. <span style="font-weight: bold;">The DIP is the primary written academic deliverable for students this term.</span> You can find all the details in the [Capstone guidelines]({{< var capstone-guidelines >}}){style="color: #0066cc;"} and read some sample DIPs from previous years [here]({{< var sample-dips >}}){style="color: #0066cc;"}. The format has been slightly updated this year, but these examples are still useful to have in mind.

- In week 5, advisors will review a complete draft of their group's DIP, but students might contact you with questions or feedback before this. 

- Students can and should keep working on data analysis and deliverables while writing their DIP! 
:::

<br>


<span style="font-weight: bold;">In 411A Today</span> [(slides @ website)]({{< var winter-week2-website >}}){style="color: #0066cc;"}

- Each group gave a 3-minute presentation with an overview of their project and a graph showing preliminary data exploration

- [Computing resources presentation]({{< var computer-resources-presentation >}}){style="color: #0066cc;"} - Kat Le, Bren School’s Technical Applications Manager, covering Google groups, shiny app deployment, and other computer resources

- Each team created a GitHub organization where they will host their project’s code repositories. We also explored GitHub issues, milestones, and projects as a tool for project management.

- Overview of DIP’s Problem Statement and Objectives and Products and Deliverables sections, and group work.

<br>

<span style="font-weight: bold;">Tasks all groups are expected to complete before next 411A class:</span>

1. Due {{< var website-project-abstract-deadline >}}: Summary paragraph for Bren website with advisor approval
2. Due {{< var winter-w3-class-date >}}: Team Management Plan draft
3. Due {{< var winter-w3-class-date >}}: Draft of DIP Team Management Plan, DIP Summary, Objectives, and Deliverables sections.
4. Fill out a [questionnaire about their Data Management Plan]({{< var rds-questionnaire-doc >}}){style="color: #0066cc;"} in preparation for meeting with the Research Data Services from UCSB’s Library this week during the 411 class.
- Continue working on independent data exploration and project implementation!



## Week 3

Hello MEDS Capstone Advisors, 

I hope you had a great long weekend.

This is your weekly EDS 411 - MEDS Capstone project newsletter. Please feel free to reach out with any questions or suggestions.

Best wishes, 

Carmen 
 
{{< include /_email_signature.qmd >}}

---

[EDS 411 Course website]({{< var 411-course-website >}}){style="color: #0066cc;"}

**[Capstone guidelines]({{< var capstone-guidelines >}}){style="color: #0066cc;"}**

<span style="font-weight: bold;">Upcoming Major Deadlines </span>

![](/411a_winter/images/411a_deadlines2.png)

::: {style="border: 2px solid #ff6600; padding: 15px; background-color: #fff5e6; border-radius: 5px;"}
<span style="font-weight: bold;">Advisor Check-ins</span>

- Students are working on the DIP Solution Design section by creating a roadmap for their project, outlining major steps, and identifying gaps in the process. They’re also planning a timeline for completion. They might reach out to you for feedback on these sections or questions about the next steps in the project.

- <span style="font-weight: bold;">At this point, all groups should have access to the project’s data.</span>

- Students shouldn’t wait for your DIP planning to be finalized to jump into coding and exploring the data! While writing the DIP, they should be actively engaging with the data, running preliminary analyses, and coming up with questions. They know this already, but <span style="font-weight: bold;">please encourage them to divide tasks among team members to make progress on multiple fronts.</span>

:::

<br>

<span style="font-weight: bold;">In 411A Today</span> [(slides @ website)]({{< var winter-week3-website >}}){style="color: #0066cc;"}

- We discussed how to create a diagram outlining their project’s development. These will be part of the DIP’s Solution Design and Timeline sections.

- We went over a strategy to create an elevator pitch for their project.

- The Research Data Services group from the UCSB Library met individually with each team to discuss their data management and archival needs as part of students’ preparation for writing their DIP Data Management Plan.

- Students worked independently with their capstone team and I checked in with each team for ~10 minutes.


<span style="font-weight: bold;">Tasks all groups are expected to complete before next 411A class:</span>

1. Due {{< var winter-w4-class-date >}}: Draft of DIP Solution Design section, including Approach, Methods, and Research Data.
2. Due {{< var winter-w4-class-date >}}: Draft DIP Timeline & Milestones including a [Gantt chart]({{< var dip-timeline-and-milestones >}}){style="color: #0066cc;"} to show their expected timeline
3. Due {{< var winter-w4-class-date >}}: Add project summary to their GitHub organization and delete demo repos
4. Prepare a flash talk to be presented next class on {{< var winter-w4-class-date >}}. 


## Week 4

Hello MEDS Capstone Advisors, 

I hope you are doing well. 

This is your weekly EDS 411 - MEDS Capstone project newsletter. Please feel free to reach out with any questions, comments, or suggestions.

Best wishes, 

Carmen 

{{< include /_email_signature.qmd >}}

---

[EDS 411 Course website]({{< var 411-course-website >}}){style="color: #0066cc;"}

**[Capstone guidelines]({{< var capstone-guidelines >}}){style="color: #0066cc;"}**

<span style="font-weight: bold;">Upcoming Major Deadlines </span>

![](/411a_winter/images/411a_deadlines2.png)

::: {style="border: 2px solid #ff6600; padding: 15px; background-color: #fff5e6; border-radius: 5px;"}
<span style="font-weight: bold;">Advisor Check-ins</span>

- Students are working on writing a Data Management Plan. They might contact you for feedback on this section or questions about how they will handle data, particularly intermediate data or results.

- The complete DIP draft is due next week on {{< var dip-draft-deadline >}}. Please remind students that submitting a complete, high-quality draft right now will allow them to devote more energy to the project implementation in the second half of the quarter. 

:::

<br>

<span style="font-weight: bold;">In 411A Today</span> [(slides @ website)]({{< var winter-week4-website >}}){style="color: #0066cc;"}

- Each group gave a 3-minute presentation that included an elevator pitch for their project, a list of deliverables, and a diagram outlining their roadmap to project completion. Students received encouragement, constructive feedback, and questions from the whole group. 

- The UCSB Associate Ombuds, Dr. David Rasch, led a workshop on resolving conflict. 

- Students worked independently with their capstone team and I checked in with each team for ~5 minutes.    


<span style="font-weight: bold;">Tasks all groups are expected to complete before next 411A class:</span>

1. Due {{< var winter-w5-class-date >}}: Complete midterm personal reflection and self/peer evaluation.
2. Due {{< var dip-draft-deadline >}}: Draft remaining DIP sections. 
3. Keep working on exploring and implementing their projects!


## Week 5

Hello MEDS Capstone Advisors, 

I hope you are doing well. 

This is your weekly EDS 411 - MEDS Capstone project newsletter. Please feel free to reach out with any questions, comments, or suggestions.

Best wishes, 

Carmen 

{{< include /_email_signature.qmd >}}

---

[EDS 411 Course website]({{< var 411-course-website >}}){style="color: #0066cc;"}

**[Capstone guidelines]({{< var capstone-guidelines >}}){style="color: #0066cc;"}**

<span style="font-weight: bold;">Upcoming Major Deadlines </span>

![](/411a_winter/images/411a_deadlines2.png)

::: {style="border: 2px solid #ff6600; padding: 15px; background-color: #fff5e6; border-radius: 5px;"}
<span style="font-weight: bold;">Advisor Check-ins</span>

- <span style="font-weight: bold;">Students will submit a complete draft of their Design and Implementation Plan (DIP) this Friday. Expect an email with a link to a Google doc on {{< var dip-draft-deadline >}}. Feedback from advisors is due on {{< var feedback-on-dip-draft-deadline >}}.</span>

- The DIP is both the primary academic deliverable this quarter and a concrete plan for how the team will address the client’s problem. A complete, high-quality draft now will provide a clear roadmap for the capstone and allow teams to focus on implementation in the coming weeks. You can see [sample past DIPs here]({{< var sample-dips >}}){style="color: #0066cc;"}.


- Most teams have a strong sense of their project direction, but some need to further add details to their methods. If you are serving as a client, before {{< var dip-draft-deadline >}} is a great time to check in with your team to confirm deliverables and objectives, ensure shared expectations, and discuss detailed implementation.

- Starting next week, we’ll switch from writing the DIP to preparing for faculty review presentations. During faculty reviews, students must present preliminary results and/or a prototype. Students might contact you to discuss what will be feasible to present on {{< var faculty-reviews-date >}}.
:::

<br> 

<span style="font-weight: bold;">In 411A Today</span> [(slides @ website)]({{< var winter-week5-website >}}){style="color: #0066cc;"}

- Groups worked on the DIP Testing Plan section, discussing how their data, code, analyses, models, and data products could break and what they could do to prevent that or test their reliability.

- Alex Phillips, Asst. Teaching Professor at Bren, led a workshop about [strategies for a successful Q&A session]({{< var faculty-reviews-strategies >}}) during faculty reviews.

- Jamie Montgomery, the MEDS program director, presented the [logistics for the faculty reviews]({{< var faculty-review-logistics-slides >}}). 

- Students worked independently with their capstone team.

<span style="font-weight: bold;">Tasks all groups are expected to complete before next 411A class:</span>

- Prepare a faculty review practice presentation (15 minutes). Students should discuss their slides with their advisor before presenting them!

- Send a single-document draft of the DIP with any comments made so far resolved to advisor and 411 instructor.

- Advance their prototype and preliminary results.

---

## Week 6

Hello MEDS Capstone Advisors, 

I hope you are doing well. 

This is your weekly EDS 411 - MEDS Capstone project newsletter. Please feel free to reach out with any questions, comments, or suggestions.

Best wishes, 

Carmen 

{{< include /_email_signature.qmd >}}

---

[EDS 411 Course website]({{< var 411-course-website >}}){style="color: #0066cc;"}

**[Capstone guidelines]({{< var capstone-guidelines >}}){style="color: #0066cc;"}**

<span style="font-weight: bold;">Upcoming Major Deadlines </span>
![](/411a_winter/images/411a_deadlines3.png)

::: {style="border: 2px solid #ff6600; padding: 15px; background-color: #fff5e6; border-radius: 5px;"}
<span style="font-weight: bold;">Advisor Check-ins</span>

- Each capstone group sent their advisor a link to their draft Design and Implementation Plan (DIP) last Friday. <span style="font-weight: bold;">Feedback from advisors on this document is due this {{< var feedback-on-dip-draft-deadline >}}.</span> I’ve read a previous draft of most sections and will give them broad feedback in this revision after you have added your comments.

- Remember, the DIP should be a *concrete and realistic* statement of what the group will do to solve the assigned problem for their client. Please encourage them to be *specific and detailed*. For context about each section of the DIP, please refer to the [Capstone guidelines]({{< var capstone-guidelines >}}){style="color: #0066cc;"}.
 
- Faculty reviews will take place on {{< var faculty-reviews-date >}} ([schedule with reviewers]({{< var faculty-review-schedule >}}{style="color: #0066cc;"})). They will consist of a 15-minute presentation + 25-minute discussion with two Bren faculty reviewers providing feedback ([rubric]({{< var faculty-review-rubric >}})). This is an opportunity for students to 1) obtain feedback about their objectives, approach, and methods and 2) show they have completed steps towards building their deliverables. 

- Help your students prepare for their presentations by discussing their slides with them and allocating time fior a practice presentation. <span style="font-weight: bold;">Student presentations should have a clear exposition of the problem or knowledge gap to be addressed, detailed objectives, and concrete deliverables or research questions!</span> 

:::

<br>

<span style="font-weight: bold;">In 411A Today</span> [(slides @ website)]({{< var winter-week6-website >}}){style="color: #0066cc;"}

- Each group gave a practice faculty review presentation (15 minutes) and received feedback from their peers and myself (5 minutes). 
- Each group will receive a document from me with feedback about their slides.
- Groups worked independently on their projects for half of class time. 


<span style="font-weight: bold;">Tasks all groups are expected to complete before next 411A class:</span>

- Prepare version 2.0 of their faculty review presentation. 
- Discuss which kind of questions they will get and prepare for answering them. Review Alex Phillips presentation on [strategies for a successful Q&A session]({{< var faculty-reviews-strategies >}}){style="color: #0066cc;"}.
- Keep working on updating their DIP.
- Discuss in their next internal team meeting what is going well and what could be improved in how they’re working as a team.
- Advance their prototype and preliminary results.


<!-- - With faculty reviews coming up, students may start feeling increased pressure, which can sometimes lead to stress-related team challenges. We <span style="font-weight: bold;">strongly encourage you to have short 10-minute individual meetings with the team members to check in and offer advice.</span> -->

---

## Week 7

Hello MEDS Capstone Advisors, 

I hope you are doing well and had a restful long weekend. 

This is your weekly EDS 411 - MEDS Capstone project newsletter. Please feel free to reach out with any questions, comments, or suggestions.

Best wishes, 

Carmen 

{{< include /_email_signature.qmd >}}

---

[EDS 411 Course website]({{< var 411-course-website >}}){style="color: #0066cc;"}

[Capstone guidelines]({{< var capstone-guidelines >}}){style="color: #0066cc;"}

<span style="font-weight: bold;">Upcoming Major Deadlines </span>
![](/411a_winter/images/411a_deadlines4.png)

<span style="font-weight: bold;">Faculty Review Documents </span>

- [Full schedule with rooms and reviewers]({{< var faculty-review-schedule >}}){style="color: #0066cc;"}
- [Presentation rubric for reviewers]({{< var faculty-review-rubric >}}){style="color: #0066cc;"}


::: {style="border: 2px solid #ff6600; padding: 15px; background-color: #fff5e6; border-radius: 5px;"}
<span style="font-weight: bold;">Advisor Check-ins</span>

- <span style="font-weight: bold;">Help your students prepare for their presentations by discussing their slides with them and allocating time during a team meeting to see a practice presentation</span>. 

- While reviewing their presentations, please ensure they <span style="font-weight: bold;">clearly communicate a defined problem statement, concrete objectives, and detailed methods</span>. A few groups are still figuring this out and will benefit from their advisor's guidance.

- For most groups, their presentation’s weak points coincide with vague portions of their DIP. <span style="font-weight: bold;">Asking students questions to identify conceptual gaps</span> is a great way to help them practice for the discussion with their reviewers and deepen their understanding of the project.

- Students are encouraged (but not required) to send their complete, updated, and revised DIP draft to their faculty reviewers. <span style="font-weight: bold;">Please check in with your team to see if they plan to send their DIP to reviewers</span> and encourage them to send the best version they can at this time.

- 🎉 Join us for a celebratory toast on Decker’s Deck (weather permitting) at 3:35 pm after the presentations. It’d be great to see you there! 

::: 

<br>

<span style="font-weight: bold;">In 411A Yesterday</span> [(slides @ website)]({{< var winter-week7-website >}}){style="color: #0066cc;"}

- Each group gave a practice faculty review presentation (15 minutes) and received feedback and answered questions from their peers and myself (5 minutes). 
- Each group will receive a document from me with feedback about their presentation.
- Groups worked independently on their projects for half of class time. 



<span style="font-weight: bold;">Tasks all groups are expected to complete before next 411A class:</span>

- Add presentation slides to shared folder. 
- Prepare for faculty reviews presentation and Q&A session. 
- Keep working on updating their DIP.
- Advance their prototype and preliminary results.


## Week 8

Hello MEDS Capstone Advisors, 

I hope you are doing well and had a good weekend.

This is your weekly EDS 411 - MEDS Capstone project newsletter. Please feel free to reach out with any questions, comments, or suggestions.

Best wishes, 

Carmen 

{{< include /_email_signature.qmd >}}

---

[EDS 411 Course website]({{< var 411-course-website >}}){style="color: #0066cc;"}

[Capstone guidelines]({{< var capstone-guidelines >}}){style="color: #0066cc;"}

<span style="font-weight: bold;">Upcoming Major Deadlines </span>
![](/411a_winter/images/411a_deadlines5.png)

::: {style="border: 2px solid #ff6600; padding: 15px; background-color: #fff5e6; border-radius: 5px;"}
<span style="font-weight: bold;">Advisor Check-ins</span>

- <span style="font-weight: bold;">Faculty review presentations went well!</span> Thank you for helping students prepare for them and advising them through their capstone projects. It’s great seeing how much students have progressed in their project planning and how they're presentations evolved.

- All teams received  feedback and questions from their faculty reviewers. <span style="font-weight: bold;">Please discuss comments and questions from the reviewers with your team.</span> Students will receive the feedback forms from reviewers later this week and they all took notes during the Q&A. 

- For the last three weeks of the quarter, we expect students to assess the feedback received during their review, incorporate any if appropriate, and continue advancing their projects. Methodological updates should be reflected in the final Design and Implementation Plan.

- <span style="font-weight: bold;">To provide you with more information about the MEDS Capstone process during Spring and check in about how projects are going, the MEDS capstone team scheduled an online meeting on Friday, March 20, 12-1 pm.</span> You should have received a calendar invite and we hope you can join!
:::

<br>

<span style="font-weight: bold;">In 411A Yesterday</span>

- We had the faculty reviews :)

<span style="font-weight: bold;">Tasks all groups are expected to complete before next 411A class:</span>

- Discuss feedback from faculty reviews with team and advisor, follow up with reviewers as needed
- Continue project implementation


## Week 9

Hello MEDS Capstone Advisors, 

I hope you are doing well and had a good weekend.

This is your weekly EDS 411 - MEDS Capstone project newsletter. Please feel free to reach out with any questions, comments, or suggestions.

Best wishes, 

Carmen 

{{< include /_email_signature.qmd >}}

---

[EDS 411 Course website]({{< var 411-course-website >}}){style="color: #0066cc;"}

[Capstone guidelines]({{< var capstone-guidelines >}}){style="color: #0066cc;"}

<span style="font-weight: bold;">Upcoming Major Deadlines </span>
![](/411a_winter/images/411a_deadlines5.png)

::: {style="border: 2px solid #ff6600; padding: 15px; background-color: #fff5e6; border-radius: 5px;"}
<span style="font-weight: bold;">Advisor Check-ins</span>

- Please help students assess and integrate feedback from their faculty reviews. <span style="font-weight: bold;">It is ok for projects to evolve, adapt, or reduce their scope at this stage.</span> Their final Design and Implementation Plan should be updated accordingly to reflect any methodological changes. 

- The <span style="font-weight: bold;">MEDS Capstone Advising pre-Spring meeting</span> will be next Friday, March 20, 12-1 pm. We'll be updating you on what to expect next quarter and hold an open discussion on how projects are going. Please join via Zoom if you can attend! Link: [https://ucsb.zoom.us/j/81979753909](https://ucsb.zoom.us/j/81979753909).
:::

<br>


<span style="font-weight: bold;">In 411A Today</span> [(slides @ website)]({{< var winter-week9-website >}}){style="color: #0066cc;"}

- Faculty reviews group debrief 
- Discussion about "the groan zone" in a creative project and strategies to productively move through it
- Groups worked independently on their projects


<span style="font-weight: bold;">Tasks all groups are expected to complete before next 411A class:</span>

1. Due {{< var winter-w10-class-date >}}: Complete end-of-quarter personal reflection.
2. Due {{< var winter-deliverables-final-date >}}: Discuss feedback from faculty reviews and update DIP accordingly.
3. Keep working on implementing their projects!


## Week 10

Hello MEDS Capstone Advisors, 

I hope you are doing well and the end of the quarter is going well for you!

This is your weekly EDS 411 - MEDS Capstone project newsletter. Please feel free to reach out with any questions, comments, or suggestions.

Best wishes, 

Carmen 

{{< include /_email_signature.qmd >}}

---

[EDS 411 Course website]({{< var 411-course-website >}}){style="color: #0066cc;"}

[Capstone guidelines]({{< var capstone-guidelines >}}){style="color: #0066cc;"}

<span style="font-weight: bold;">Upcoming Major Deadlines </span>
![](/411a_winter/images/411a_deadlines5.png)

::: {style="border: 2px solid #ff6600; padding: 15px; background-color: #fff5e6; border-radius: 5px;"}
<span style="font-weight: bold;">Advisor Check-ins</span>

- <span style="font-weight: bold;">All Winter quarter deliverables are due by the end of this week (see upcoming deadlines above). </span>

- <span style="font-weight: bold;">You should receive one Peer/Self evaluation email per student this week. Please read them and, if needed, take action on the comments provided by your students.</span> If everything is going great, give them kudos for the good work! If there's some negative dynamics brewing, you can offer to help. Be mindful of timing - depending on the evaluations, it might be best to talk with your team during week 1 of the Spring quarter so they can implement any corrective action. 


- The <span style="font-weight: bold;">MEDS Capstone Advising pre-Spring meeting</span> will be next Friday, March 20, 12-1 pm. We'll be updating you on what to expect next quarter and hold an open discussion on how projects are going. Please join via Zoom if you can attend! Link: [https://ucsb.zoom.us/j/81979753909](https://ucsb.zoom.us/j/81979753909).
:::

<br>

<span style="font-weight: bold;">In 411A Today</span> [(slides @ website)]({{< var winter-week10-website >}}){style="color: #0066cc;"}

- Students held a post-mortem meeting to discuss how their team worked during winter quarter. The goal was to identify lessons learned and ideas to improve going forward. 
- We went over what to expect in 411B: fewer lectures and brainstorms, more peer testing, more dedicated work time, continuing presentation and public speaking practice. 
- Students established expectations for work during spring break and finals week and about when they will resume their project.
- I asked everyone to schedule their team and advisor weekly meetings and they should revise their DIP timeline and discuss it with their advisors at the latest on week 1 or Spring quarter. 

<span style="font-weight: bold;">Tasks all groups are expected to complete before the end of this week ({{< var winter-deliverables-final-date >}})</span>

1. Send final DIP to 411 instructor, advisor, and client.
2. Submit Peer/Self evaluation form and send a copy to their advisor.
3. Finish set up for Spring quarter. 
4. Fill out UCSB 411A course survey. 


## Finals Week

Hi MEDS Capstone Advisors, 


I hope the end of quarter is going smoothly for you all! 

This is the last EDS 411 - MEDS Capstone project newsletter for the quarter. Please feel free to reach out with any questions, comments, concerns, or suggestions. We’ll resume the newsletter in the Spring quarter.

Thank you and I wish you a re-energizing Spring break!

Carmen 

{{< include /_email_signature.qmd >}}

---

---

[EDS 411 Course website]({{< var 411-course-website >}}){style="color: #0066cc;"}

[Capstone guidelines]({{< var capstone-guidelines >}}){style="color: #0066cc;"}

::: {style="border: 2px solid #ff6600; padding: 15px; background-color: #fff5e6; border-radius: 5px;"}
<span style="font-weight: bold;">Advisor Check-ins</span>

- <span style="font-weight: bold;">You should have received the final edit of the Design and Implementation Plan (DIP).</span> No further action is needed. Please let me know ASAP if your team still needs to send you the DIP.

- <span style="font-weight: bold;">I'll email you the proposed grades for students in your group.</span> If you have any comments, please let me know by Thursday. I’ll submit grades for EDS 411A on Friday. 

- The <span style="font-weight: bold;">MEDS Capstone Advising pre-Spring meeting</span> will be this Friday, March 20, 12-1 pm. We'll be updating you on what to expect next quarter and hold an open discussion on how projects are going. Please join via Zoom if you can attend! Link: [https://ucsb.zoom.us/j/81979753909](https://ucsb.zoom.us/j/81979753909).

:::
